reviewer_objection: Why should Table 1A be read as an eight-row document-depth facet rather than as a flat count of authoritative cases?
bounded_response_now: Because the current authoritative package contains materially different support depths inside the same high-authority family: six rows have direct document text, one row has a captured charging document but incomplete caption normalization, and one row remains agency-summary-only without court-facing text. Table 1A is publishable precisely because it makes those depth differences visible instead of flattening them.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: no flat authoritative-count rhetoric and no equivalence between direct text, partial normalization, and summary-only support.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Table 1A legend; authority-depth inset; reviewer-safe methods/results panel.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Keep the 6 / 1 / 1 split explicit whenever Table 1A is reused.

reviewer_objection: Why is LIMEN-000002 still treated as a weak row if official EEOC materials and a civil-action number are already present?
bounded_response_now: Because the current package has agency summaries about filing and settlement/decree progression, but it still lacks a captured court-facing complaint, decree, docket entry, or judicial order text. Agency-reported litigation posture is useful, but it is not the same thing as court-originating document support.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: no agency-summary-to-court-text inflation and no implied merits finding.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Court-gap badge; blocker ladder; authority-funnel limitation row.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Capture one genuinely different public court-facing complaint, decree, docket entry, or order, or keep the row explicitly summary-grade.

reviewer_objection: Why is LIMEN-000005 not promoted to the same document-grade class as the six direct-text rows if the indictment PDF is already captured?
bounded_response_now: Because the local package supports accusation-stage wording and document presence, but exact caption normalization is still partial. The current OCR/public-source state recovers only bounded caption fragments, so the row is useful for procedural-posture and methods claims, not for fully normalized exact docket citation.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: no false caption precision and no conversion of an accusation-stage row into a fully normalized court-document anchor.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Normalization-stage badge; citation-precision note; partial-document caution row.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Use a materially different OCR stack or another public exact-caption source before upgrading the row.

reviewer_objection: Why does Figure 8 include eleven authoritative rows while Table 1A includes only eight?
bounded_response_now: Because the two visuals answer different questions. Figure 8 is an authority-geography concentration panel and therefore includes the full 11-row authoritative core, including the bounded Slovenian official-project-page follow-through row for diversification logic and the later FTC core additions. Table 1A is narrower: it is an 8-row document-depth facet for the shared authoritative package and therefore excludes the ORION follow-through row from its denominator.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: no denominator drift between the eight-row Table 1A facet and the eleven-row authoritative core/Figure 8 panel.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Table 1A vs Figure 8 denominator note; geography-diversification badge; core-versus-facet guardrail.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Keep ORION bounded to geography-diversification and official-follow-through wording only.

reviewer_objection: Is Table 1A publishable if two of the eight rows are still visibly incomplete or blocked?
bounded_response_now: Yes, but as an evidence-architecture contribution rather than as a completeness claim. The table is useful because it shows six quote-ready document-grade rows alongside one summary-only court-gap row and one normalization-limited charging row with explicit upgrade conditions. That heterogeneity is part of the result, not a defect to hide.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Yes: the package stays a methods/results support-depth contribution, not a court-completeness or prevalence claim.
visualization_or_dashboard_hook: Reviewer-control panel; thesis evidence-depth appendix; support-depth dashboard inset.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Prefer one real LIMEN-000002 court-text gain before any stronger Table 1A rhetoric.
